prompt:
The nightly till-roll import (rs-tillroll crate) has stopped importing anything and I cannot see why. Symptom one: every export gets bounced with "not a till roll" — including files I watched the terminal write minutes earlier. I opened one in the editor and the first line reads CAFÉ TILL v2, exactly what we compare against, byte-for-byte identical as far as I can tell. Symptom two, discovered while poking at symptom one: if I comment out the header check locally and shove a roll through, it imports ZERO items — from a roll with a dozen ITEM lines on it. The lines look completely ordinary, `ITEM;Flat White;2;3.80` and the like, and our little field matcher passes its own unit tests, so I don't think the matcher engine itself is broken.

So: real exports rejected at the door, and even past the door every priced line is treated as terminal chatter and skipped. Both smell related but I've been wrong before. cargo test --offline runs the whole story (tests/tillroll.rs); make it pass without touching tests/, Cargo.toml, or .gitignore — and keep the header check itself, it's wanted; my commenting-it-out was just for poking around.
